proposals.architecture_gap: The current AAS runtime still needs a stable end-state architecture that can run self-improvement cycles without uncontrolled drift.
proposals.data_model_changes: Add convergence-aware cycle metrics, pressure-test traces, adversarial review traces, and freeze-state metadata.
proposals.governance_implications: Council deliberation now includes convergence and freeze decisions.
proposals.invention_concept: Promote the bootstrap overlay into a durable service kernel with stronger recovery orchestration and freeze-state discipline.
proposals.migration_strategy: Promote only the strongest, pressure-tested, adversarial-reviewed architecture into the next CSSM version.
proposals.problem_statement: Pending architecture pressure remains on: Apply merged delta for Cycle 100 Meta-Merge Architecture, Apply selected architecture for Cycle 101 Converged Meta-Architecture.
proposals.proposed_architecture_change: Promote the bootstrap overlay into a durable service kernel with stronger recovery orchestration and freeze-state discipline.
proposals.protocol_implications: The PAEE loop becomes bounded by convergence and explicit finalization rather than running indefinitely.
proposals.security_implications: Pressure and adversarial traces become explicit inputs to architecture selection.
proposals.title: Cycle 102 Runtime Stability Spine
